title: GTM Baseline Evaluation Scores
description: Repeatable scoring framework for end-to-end GTM workflow evaluation. Each test run produces one file conforming to this schema.

test_id.description: Unique identifier for this test run (e.g., 'baseline-001')

test_date.description: ISO 8601 date of the test run

test_subject.description: Company/business used as the test subject (e.g., 'unitedarts.cz')

system_version.description: Git commit hash or tag identifying the system version under test

steps.description: Per-step evaluation results, one entry per workflow step executed

comparison.description: Optional comparison against a previous test run

step_id.description: Stable identifier for this step (never changes between runs)

step_number.description: Sequential step number in the workflow

phase.description: Which flywheel phase this step belongs to

llm_outputs.description: LLM-generated outputs produced at this step

screenshots.description: Paths to screenshots captured during this step

step_scores.description: Five-dimension scoring for each workflow step

availability.description: Does this feature exist and work? 0=missing, 5=partial/buggy, 10=fully working as designed

seamlessness.description: How smooth is the transition from the previous step? 10=automatic, 7=AI suggests next step, 5=user must navigate manually, 3=requires multiple clicks/pages, 0=broken transition

proactiveness.description: Does the system anticipate the next action? 10=auto-proceeds with explanation, 7=suggests concrete next action, 5=shows generic guidance, 3=waits passively, 0=no indication of what to do next

ai_quality.description: Quality of AI-generated content. null if step has no AI output. 10=excellent, specific, actionable, 7=good with minor issues, 5=acceptable but generic, 3=poor quality needs significant editing, 0=unusable

user_effort_detail.description: Breakdown of user effort components (inputs to the user_effort score formula)

extra_prompts.description: Chat messages beyond the minimum 1 approve/reject per gate. Does NOT count the initial business description or required approvals.

manual_corrections.description: Times the user had to fix AI-generated output (edit a message, correct a strategy section, remap a column)

manual_workarounds.description: Steps the user did manually that should have been automated (copy-paste data, navigate to a different page to trigger something, manually enter data the system should know)

unnecessary_clarifications.description: Times the system asked something it should already know from context (re-asking business description, asking which contacts when there's only one batch)

total_user_messages.description: Total user messages/actions at this step (for reference, not scored)

total_clicks.description: Total UI clicks required at this step (for reference, not scored)

llm_output.description: Evaluation of a single LLM-generated output

input_summary.description: Brief description of what was sent to the LLM

output_summary.description: Brief description of what the LLM produced

raw_output.description: The actual LLM output text (for future analysis and comparison)

token_cost.description: Token/credit cost of this LLM call

llm_scores.description: Five-dimension quality scoring for LLM outputs

relevance.description: Does the output fit the context? 10=perfectly relevant to unitedarts.cz and event agencies, 5=somewhat related, 1=completely off-topic

accuracy.description: Factually correct? 10=all facts verified, 7=minor inaccuracies, 5=some errors but usable, 3=significant errors, 1=mostly wrong

specificity.description: Specific to this business, not generic? 10=deeply specific (mentions unitedarts.cz services, Czech market details), 5=could apply to any entertainment company, 1=completely generic

actionability.description: Can you act on it immediately? 10=ready to use as-is, 7=needs minor tweaks, 5=needs significant editing, 3=only directional, 1=not actionable

tone.description: Appropriate for B2B outreach? 10=professional, warm, not salesy, 7=good but slightly off, 5=acceptable but generic tone, 3=too aggressive/too casual, 1=inappropriate

severity.description: How much this impacted the workflow

recovery.description: How was it resolved? (e.g., 'page refresh', 'manual workaround', 'could not recover')

aggregate_scores.description: Aggregate scores computed from all steps. Each is the arithmetic mean of the corresponding per-step score.

overall_completeness.description: Mean of all availability scores. Measures: how much of the ideal workflow actually exists and works?

workflow_seamlessness.description: Mean of all seamlessness scores. Measures: how smooth is the end-to-end flow?

ai_quality.description: Mean of all non-null ai_quality scores. Measures: how good is the AI-generated content?

user_effort.description: Mean of all user_effort scores. Higher = less effort. Measures: how much extra work does the user have to do?

proactiveness.description: Mean of all proactiveness scores. Measures: does the system anticipate next steps?

llm_quality.description: Aggregate LLM output quality across all steps

total_errors.description: Total errors encountered across all steps

total_blockers.description: Total blocker-severity errors (steps that could not complete)

steps_completed.description: Number of steps that reached 'completed' status

llm_aggregate_scores.description: Mean of all LLM output scores across all steps

total_outputs_scored.description: Number of LLM outputs that were scored

total_token_cost.description: Total token/credit cost across all LLM calls

comparison_data.description: Delta comparison against a baseline or previous run

deltas.description: Score differences (current - baseline) for each aggregate

new_errors.description: Errors in current run that were not in baseline

fixed_errors.description: Errors from baseline that are now resolved

duration_seconds.description: Total wall-clock time for the entire test run

video_path.description: Path to recorded video of the test run

notes.description: General notes about the test run
